name: Set
summary: Deprecated. In SketchUp 2014 this class was changed from Set to Sketchup::Set in order to avoid conflict with the Ruby Standard Library. The Sketchup::Set class is deprecated and new extensions should make use of Ruby's Set class unless they need backward compatibility. The set class represents a collection of unique objects. This class is useful for keeping track of a group of related entities, kind of like a selection set that stays around for as long as you need it to. To make a set of your own, create an empty one using Sketchup::Set.new, and then add items to it.

methods.name: clear
methods.summary: The clear method is used to clear all objects out of the set.
methods.remarks: The clear method is used to clear all objects out of the set.

methods.name: contains?
methods.summary: The #contains? method is an alias for #include?.
methods.remarks: The #contains? method is an alias for #include?.

methods.name: delete
methods.summary: The delete object is used to delete or remove an object from the set.
methods.remarks: The delete object is used to delete or remove an object from the set.

returns.doc: object - the object that was deleted.

methods.name: each
methods.summary: The each method is used to iterate through all of the objects in the set.
methods.remarks: The each method is used to iterate through all of the objects in the set.

methods.name: empty?
methods.summary: The empty? method is used to determine whether the set is empty.
methods.remarks: The empty? method is used to determine whether the set is empty.

returns.doc: status - true if the set is empty, false if it is not empty.

methods.name: include?
methods.summary: The #include? method is used to determine if the set includes a particular object.
methods.remarks: The #include? method is used to determine if the set includes a particular object.

methods.name: insert
methods.summary: The insert method is used to insert an object into the set.
methods.remarks: The insert method is used to insert an object into the set.

params.name: object
params.doc: The object to be inserted into the set.

returns.doc: size - the number of objects in the set

methods.name: length
methods.summary: The #length method is an alias for #size.
methods.remarks: The #length method is an alias for #size.

methods.name: size
methods.summary: The #size method is used to determine the number of objects in the set.
methods.remarks: The #size method is used to determine the number of objects in the set.

methods.name: to_a
methods.summary: The to_a method is used to get an Array of the entities in your Set.
methods.remarks: The to_a method is used to get an Array of the entities in your Set.

returns.doc: array - The Array of the entities in the Set.
